title: CHEM 109 - General Chemistry I
description: Fundamental concepts: chemical foundations, stoichiometry, chemical reactions, gases, thermochemistry, atomic theory, bonding, liquids, solutions. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: CHEM 110 - General Chemistry II
description: Fundamental concepts: kinetics; equilibrium; acids and bases; acid-base, solubility, and complex ion equilibria; entropy and free energy; electrochemistry; qualitative analysis. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: MATH 107 - Introduction to Linear Algebra
description: Euclidean spaces, matrices and matrix arithmetic, solving systems of equations, and eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Emphasis on applications and use of computation.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: MATH 110 - Calculus II
description: Logarithmic and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, techniques of integration, infinite sequences and series, conic sections, polar coordinates.

title: MATH 210 - Calculus III
description: Vectors; parametric equations; 3-dimensional analytic geometry; vector- valued functions; partial derivatives; multiple integrals; introduction to line integrals.

title: PHYX 109 - General Physics A: Mechanics
description: Calculus-based, for science/engineering students.

title: PHYX 210 - General Physics B: Thermodynamics, Waves and Optics
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: PHYX 211 - General Physics C: Electricity, Magnetism
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: MATH 311 - Vector Calculus
description: Vector fields; line and surface integrals; Green’s theorem, divergence theorem, Stokes’ theorem; applications.

title: MATH 313 - Ordinary Differential Equations
description: Systems and series solution methods; applications. Numerical and analytical techniques.

title: PHYX 320 - Modern Physics
description: Atomic, solid state, nuclear, and particle physics.

title: PHYX 324 - Analytical Mechanics
description: Principles and foundations of mechanics, from classical to modern ideas.

title: PHYX 325 - Thermal Physics
description: Elements of classical and statistical thermodynamics.

title: PHYX 340 - Mathematical and Computational Methods
description: Numerical, symbolic and graphical programming and simulations, mathematical applications important to physicists.

title: PHYX 441 - Electricity and Magnetism I
description: Vector analysis, electrostatics, magnetostatics and electrodynamics.

title: PHYX 442 - Electricity and Magnetism II
description: Conservation laws, electromagnetic waves, potentials & fields, radiation and relativity.

title: PHYX 450 - Quantum Physics I
description: Quantum mechanics; introductory atomic physics.

title: PHYX 484 - Physics Seminar I
description: This is the first of a two-semester sequence. Students are expected to develop the skills necessary to research, prepare and effectively deliver technical presentations to an audience of peers.

title: PHYX 485 - Physics Seminar II
description: Seminar presentations by physics majors, faculty, and guest speakers. Capstone course. All physics majors are encouraged to attend the seminars.

title: PHYX 315 - Introduction to Electronics and Electronic Instrumentation
description: Devices and circuits, both analog and digital, in science instrumentation. Construct amplifiers and digital circuits.

title: PHYX 316 - Electronic Instrumentation and Control Systems
description: Design/build electronic instruments. Direct interfacing of computers.

title: PHYX 462 - Senior Lab
description: Experiments for senior physics majors. Bridge gap between carefully structured lower division lab experiences and truly independent research and development.

title: MATH 344 - Linear Algebra
description: Matrices, vector spaces, inner product spaces, linear transformations, canonical forms, eigenvalues, matrix factorization, numerical methods, applications.

title: MATH 442 - Abstract Algebra
description: An introduction to algebraic structures including groups, rings, fields, and polynomial rings. Provides a solid foundation in these topics and a foundation for further study. Provide prospective secondary teachers with a general context in which the primary notions of secondary education algebra are unified.

title: PHYX 495 - Undergraduate Research
description: Individual investigation of selected problem.
